div.p: William, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his enitre land, present and future, greeting. Know that I have given and conceded Inverarity to Orm son of Hugh by its correct boundaries, for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, freely and quitly, saving to me my service that pertains to that land. I also strictly enjoin that wherever he may find neyfs and fugitives beloning to that land he may justly have them, and I strictly forbid that anyone should unjustly withhold any of these from him, upon my prohibition. As witnesses: Nicholas the chancellor; Earl Waldef; Walter son of Alan, the steward; Ness son of Williaml; Richard Comyn; Gilbert son of Richer; William de la Haye; Walter Berkeley. At Forfar.
